title: QA Male/Female Lattice Balance — m=9 is the last exact 2/3 modulus
_fixture_purpose: Anchor fixture certifying that m in {3,6,9} are the only positive integers where {1,...,m}^2 has exactly 2/3 male states (b^2 < 2*e^2). The continuous-limit male fraction is 1-sqrt(2)/4 approx 0.64645. The gap between exact 2/3 and the asymptote is sqrt(2)/4 - 1/3 approx 0.02022. mod-9 orbit splits: Fibonacci 17:7, Lucas 17:7, Third 14:10, Satellite 5:3, Singularity 1:0; total 54:27 = 2:1 exact. All m >= 10 fall strictly below 2/3, verified to m=10000.
_asymptote_derivation: integral_0^1 min(1, y*sqrt(2)) dy = sqrt(2)/2 * (1/sqrt(2))^2 + (1 - 1/sqrt(2)) = 1 - sqrt(2)/4
_gap_formula: sqrt(2)/4 - 1/3; the boundary irrational sqrt(2) appears in its own asymptotic density formula

lucas._note: Fibonacci and Lucas share identical 17:7 split — unit and ramified prime in Z[phi]

third._note: Third orbit |f|=11 (split prime in Z[phi]) has different distribution

_theorem_nt: b^2 < 2*e^2 is a classification of (b,e) states — an observer output. It is never used as a QA state input or modular arithmetic operand. The T-step qa_step(b,e,m) = (e, ((b+e-1)%m)+1) makes no reference to the male/female classification.

qa_connections.boundary: b/e = sqrt(2) is the male/female boundary; equivalent to I = C-F = 2de - ba = 0 (Iverson)
qa_connections.koenig: Male states have I > 0 (inner Koenig radius positive); female states have I < 0
qa_connections.pell: Pell convergents to sqrt(2) straddle the boundary: (1,1)male, (3,2)female, (7,5)male, (17,12)female alternating with |I|=1 (minimum)
qa_connections.z_sqrt2: g(b,e) = b^2 - 2*e^2 = -I is the Z[sqrt(2)] Pell indicator; male iff g < 0
qa_connections.m9_special: m=9 is the LAST modulus where the discrete Beatty-sqrt(2) count exactly tiles 2/3; beyond m=9 the irrational nature of sqrt(2) prevents exact rational partition

sources: Ben Iverson, Pythagoras and the Quantum World Vol.1-2 (QA-1/QA-2, 1975-1996): I=C-F, male/female classification
Hardy, G.H. and Wright, E.M. (2008) An Introduction to the Theory of Numbers, 6th ed. Oxford ISBN 978-0-19-921986-5: Beatty sequences Ch.VI
Dale, W. (2026) QA Male/Female Lattice Balance — computational discovery, signal_experiments session 2026-06-04

validation_checks.description: m=3 has 6 male, m=6 has 24 male
